title: Unknown XML Objects, Zod Admission, Loss-Aware Normalization, and Provenance · Type Forensics

Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Keep parser and database JSON values unknown until discriminated format, structural, scalar, cardinality, and semantic runtime validation passes.
Build distinct validated RSS and Atom transport variants and exhaustively normalize each supported case.
Select scoped stable identity from Atom ID, RSS GUID meaning, canonical link, or bounded fingerprint and report collisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Ts Unknown Boundary
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 4244 during a cross-tenant privacy and cache audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile unknown parser values, discriminated formats, identity, provenance, and normalization loss; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep parser and database JSON values unknown until discriminated format, structural, scalar, cardinality, and semantic runtime validation passes.
